elements.title: Shorty Gallagher
elements.contents: ... that there was a decades-long search for a photograph of Shorty Gallagher (pictured)?

elements.title: an embassy in Fiji
elements.contents: ... that, by establishing diplomatic missions like an embassy in Fiji, the Philippine government hopes to play a bigger role in international politics?

elements.title: Lucie Stern
elements.contents: ... that the head of one piano department called Lucie Stern "the rarest talent of any child I ever heard"?

elements.title: a carnival group in Brazil
elements.contents: ... that a carnival group in Brazil plays songs by the Beatles in carnival march rhythms?

elements.title: Palm Beach Biltmore
elements.contents: ... that Francisco Batista, the brother of ousted president Fulgencio Batista, fled to the Palm Beach Biltmore immediately after the Cuban Revolution and stated "We'll be back after the trouble is over"?

elements.title: Chronicles from the Siege
elements.contents: ... that a review of Chronicles from the Siege called it "a meditation on the endurance and fragmentation of Palestinian life under siege"?

elements.title: Rebecca Landon
elements.contents: ... that the Shaker leader Rebecca Landon said in 1842 that she experienced a vision that warned that the United States would face divine judgment for practicing slavery?

elements.title: a 2018 data breach at British Airways
elements.contents: ... that a 2018 data breach at British Airways was traced to just 22 lines of injected JavaScript on its payment pages?

elements.title: her convent
elements.contents: ... that in 1988 a young nun was kidnapped from her convent by her own family?
